4 Quality Information Document for OMI_EXTREME_MHW_ARCTIC 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-ARC-OMI-QUID-EXTREME 
Issue 1.1 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_EXTREME_MHW_ARCTIC_area_averaged_anomalies 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
The time series provided here represents: 
• Area-averaged temperature anomaly normalized relative to the 90th percentile 
of the seasonally varying climatology for the full Barents Sea region for the 
surface and the near bottom 
A marine heatwave (MHW) index for the Barents Sea is calculated as the temperature 
deviation from the daily climatological average (1991-2020) normalized relative to the 
90th percentile, based on model reanalysis and averaged over the area 68°N - 80°N, 
18°E - 55°E. The time series of the MHW index is evaluated against normalized 
temperature anomalies from obser vations in the Kola Section in the southern Barents 
Sea (Alekseev, 2005; Karsakov et al., 2018; González-Pola et al., 2020), which represents 
the Atlantic Water inflow to the Barents Sea, and the average temperature and extent 
of Atlantic Water (T > 3 °C) as estimated from the annual Barents Sea Ecosystem Survey.
